summary: This is a brief announcement post where Joel introduces part two of his four-part series on writing functional specifications. He emphasizes that the most important aspect of product design is nailing down the user experience—defining the screens, how they work, and what they do. The post serves primarily as a teaser and navigation pointer to the full article.
key_points: Functional specifications should focus on defining the user experience above all else
Product design means nailing down screens, workflows, and functionality
This is part of a structured four-part series on writing specs
money_quotes: When you design a product, inside and out, the most important thing is to nail down the user experience.
What are the screens, how do they work, what do they do.
key_insight: The core purpose of a functional specification is to define and nail down the user experience before building anything.
